title: Can't create IdString objects in pyosys
body:
For example when getting the connection for a cell port `\Q`, one would access the `connections_` dictionary of the cell using an IdString. In the past I did this as follows:

```
conn = cell.connections_[ys.IdString("\Q")]
```

With current master (Yosys 0.9+36, git sha1 58af64b6) the creation of the IdString obect leads to an error:

```
>>> ys.IdString("\Q")
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
Boost.Python.ArgumentError: Python argument types in
    None.__init__(IdString, str)
did not match C++ signature:
    __init__(_object*)
```

It seems IdString objects can only be created from Python without supplying a string.

Is this expected behavior? Is there a different way to accomplish the functionality outlined in the beginning instead?
code_blocks:
>>> ys.IdString("\Q")
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
Boost.Python.ArgumentError: Python argument types in
    None.__init__(IdString, str)
did not match C++ signature:
    __init__(_object*)
